bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Structural Bioinformatics & Protein Science

AI-curated selection: protein structure prediction, molecular dynamics, docking, and structure-based function annotation.

21 papers curated · High: 4 · Medium: 17 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

Cells 2024 Medium

p70S6K as a Potential Anti-COVID-19 Target: Insights from Wet Bench and In Silico Studies

This study identifies the host kinase p70S6K as a potential therapeutic target for COVID-19 by showing its 3.5-fold activation in macrophages following SPIKE transfection, linking it to viral infection and cytokine storm. The findings suggest that targeting p70S6K could help mitigate long-term COVID-19 complications regardless of viral mutations.

immunologydrug-discoverymethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper introduces BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method outperforms existing tools like SAINTexpress in identifying genuine protein-protein interactions while filtering out non-specific background noise.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new deep learning method that uses a patch-based Transformer to generate and denoise full-gene expression data from single cells, overcoming the limitations of previous models that only work with a subset of highly variable genes. It preserves both global and local biological structure while being computationally efficient, enabling more comprehensive transcriptome-wide analysis.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a new pipeline that uses SPECTER document embeddings and similarity scoring to automatically rank biomedical literature for systematic reviews, specifically applied to mutational signature analysis. The method outperforms keyword-only and TF-IDF approaches in prioritizing relevant studies, potentially reducing manual screening effort.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

The authors present BBBP_Atlas, a unified computational framework that improves prediction of blood-brain barrier permeability for both small molecules and peptides by addressing data fragmentation and redundancy issues in existing datasets. The model uses interpretable representations that do not rely on predefined physicochemical descriptors or conformation-dependent features, enabling better transferability across diverse chemical modalities.

drug-discoverymachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study demonstrates that mutational biases, not just natural selection, significantly shape protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors show that the virus's mutational spectrum explains over 50% of observed amino acid substitutions and predicts long-term changes in amino acid composition across the proteome.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze transcriptomic data from TNBC patients, identifying key metabolic hub genes and pathways that are rewired in this aggressive cancer subtype. The authors pinpoint ATP1A2 as a significant prognostic marker, suggesting it could be a potential therapeutic target. The work provides a comprehensive map of metabolic coordination in TNBC, offering new insights for prognosis and treatment.

transcriptomicssystems-biologycancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational method that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of signaling hubs, relays, and network vulnerabilities. By modeling the entire signaling network rather than individual interactions, it can predict how tissues respond to perturbations like the loss of a single cell type. This approach provides a systems-level view of intercellular signaling in its native spatial context.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysismethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper presents a new approach for assessing the quality of tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data from longitudinal studies. By considering the underlying experimental data model, the authors develop metrics to guide the selection of rank and trial for Non-negative CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (NCPD) decomposition. They also demonstrate how interpreting results through a dictionary learning framework can improve the analysis.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

This paper introduces DyViA, a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to predict personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals. The platform can forecast how specific phenotypes (e.g., physiological or molecular traits) might change with age, offering a new tool for studying healthy aging.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

This paper introduces a five-dimensional functional state space framework that maps disease transcriptomes onto coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling easier comparison across conditions and human-AI interpretation. The framework organizes transcriptomic signals into five functional systems, 14 subcategories, and an infrastructure layer, implemented as a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring.

transcriptomicssystems-biologymethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper shows that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often fail to fully remove correlation between gene expression and sequencing depth, especially in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization that adjusts for this residual bias, improving differential expression analysis.

transcriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously design the sequence and 3D structure of antibodies, nanobodies, and T-cell receptors, addressing a key limitation of previous methods that handled these tasks separately. By modeling the full atomic details and binding geometry, it enables more accurate design of immune receptors for therapeutic applications.

deep-learningprotein-structuredrug-discovery
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs collectively determine cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, moving beyond existing methods that analyze programs independently. GPN reconstructs cell-specific regulatory decision-making among concurrent biological programs, providing deeper insight into cellular heterogeneity.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales to large datasets, and accounts for unknown cell types not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper introduces a framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors construct 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers to test how well agents can perform alignment tasks and diagnose their behavior through trace inspection. This work addresses the challenge of benchmarking agents on complex scientific tasks that produce hard-to-verify outputs.

spatial-omicstoolmachine-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper introduces a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs from the literature, rather than learning dense dimensions de novo. This approach produces sparse, interpretable embeddings that incorporate prior biological knowledge and reduce the need for post-hoc interpretation and batch correction.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative fitness of individual cancer cells in tumors with chromosomal instability. By modeling tumor evolution as a branching process, it can quantify which cells are more likely to drive tumor growth, even in complex scenarios like whole-genome duplication.

cancersingle-cellmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new template-free, generative AI framework that predicts polar enzymatic reaction mechanisms without relying on predefined rule-based dictionaries. It uses a flow matching model and bidirectional beam search to learn chemistry directly, enabling it to generalize to novel chemical reactions that traditional methods cannot handle.

machine-learningmethodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based method for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data by jointly modeling gene expression and spatial information. Unlike existing graph neural network approaches, BertST leverages a transformer architecture to capture both local and long-range spatial dependencies, improving domain identification accuracy on patient data.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsmachine-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses bibliometric analysis and large language models to identify the most influential recent publications in cancer genomics and diagnostics, revealing thematic shifts in topics that attract early citation prominence. The findings can help guide research strategy and investment by highlighting which areas are gaining traction.

cancermachine-learningmethodology
